This is not describing what's broken — it's prescribing the exact implementation.

None of them import the hook, render a component, or verify actual runtime behavior. The only behavioral tests are pass-to-pass (Jest, ESLint, Prettier).

The agent could read solve.sh if it exists in the container, or simply grep for the expected patterns ('mounted.current = false', 'return undefined') from the test file at tests/test_outputs.py and add them as strings to pass.

The fail-to-pass tests only check source code text patterns, not whether the hook actually skips the callback on mount in Strict Mode.
